evals.prompt: I just dropped a sprite sheet at /tmp/eval-inputs/sample-spritesheet.png — a 3x2 grid on a checkered background. Build me a sprite atlas with one animation per cell (idle, walk, run, jump, attack, hurt). Save the atlas to /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-1/.
evals.expected_output: A 6-frame sprite atlas with 6 animations at /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-1/ — atlas.png (transparent sprites), atlas.json (with 6 frame entries and 6 animation entries), and clean 256x256 PNGs in a tmp/ subfolder.
expectations: atlas.png is created and is a valid PNG image
atlas.json contains exactly 6 entries in the `frames` object
atlas.json contains exactly 6 entries in the `animations` array
Each animation references frames that exist in the frames object
The output frames have transparent backgrounds (the checkered bg is removed)

evals.prompt: I have a sprite sheet at /tmp/eval-inputs/irregular-spritesheet.png — 4 hero poses in row 1 (at x=0,200,400,600, each 200x256) plus a 'MENU' label cell at x=800. The bg is solid green (#00ff00). I want a 4-frame 'hero' animation, not the menu cell. Process the file and write the atlas to /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-2/. Use the cell rectangles, not auto-grid (the cells are uniform here but I want to make sure it works).
evals.expected_output: A 4-frame sprite atlas at /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-2/ — 4 transparent hero frames in the atlas (not 5; the MENU label is skipped), with 1 'hero' animation containing all 4 frames. No green background remains.
expectations: atlas.png is created and is a valid PNG
atlas.json contains exactly 4 frame entries (NOT 5 — the MENU cell was skipped)
atlas.json contains exactly 1 'hero' animation referencing all 4 frames
The output frames have transparent backgrounds (green was removed via chroma key)

evals.prompt: I have a single 256x256 hero PNG at /tmp/eval-inputs/green-spritesheet.png with a solid #00ff00 green background. I just need one transparent 256x256 PNG, no atlas, no animations. The hero should be the only output, with the green removed. Save the clean version to /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-3/hero.png.
evals.expected_output: A single clean transparent 256x256 PNG of the hero at /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-3/hero.png — no green background, hero is centered and fills most of the frame.
expectations: Output file exists at /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-3/hero.png
Output is exactly 256x256 px
Output has an alpha channel (RGBA mode)
Output has transparent background where the green used to be
The hero character is still visible in the output

evals.prompt: I'm making a retro pixel-art game and I have a sprite sheet at /tmp/eval-inputs/sample-spritesheet.png (the bundled one — 3 cols x 2 rows, 6 cells). I want to use grid mode to extract the 6 cells, and then re-pack the atlas at 128x128 frame size (smaller, more retro) instead of the default 256x256. Save everything to /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-4/.
evals.expected_output: A 6-frame atlas at 128x128 per frame. With --cols 3 and 6 cells, the atlas is 2 rows × 3 columns of 128x128 = 384x256. All 6 frames are transparent, packed correctly.
expectations: atlas.png is created
atlas dimensions are 384x256 (3 cols x 2 rows of 128x128 cells)
Each frame in atlas.json has dimensions 128x128
All 6 frames are present in atlas.json

evals.prompt: I have a sprite sheet at /tmp/eval-inputs/sample-spritesheet.png (768x512, 3x2 grid). I want to use grid mode with --cols 7 --rows 3 — but I'm not sure if that will work. Build the atlas at /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-5/. (Note: 7 doesn't divide 768 cleanly — this should fail with a clear error message that tells me what the closest valid grid would be.)
evals.expected_output: The command should fail with a clear, short error message like "✗ --cols 7 doesn't divide width 768 cleanly (leftover Xpx). Nearest valid: --cols 8 --rows 3." — NOT a stack trace, NOT silent success with bogus frames.
expectations: Process exits with non-zero status
Error message does NOT contain 'node_modules' or stack-trace paths
Error message mentions '--cols' or 'grid' and the leftover pixel count
Error message suggests a valid (cols, rows) alternative

evals.prompt: I have a sprite sheet at /tmp/eval-inputs/green-spritesheet.png (256x256) and I want to extract a single cell at (x=300, y=0, w=100, h=100) using mapped mode. Save the extracted cell to /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-6/. (Note: x=300 is outside the 256-wide image — this should fail with a clear bounds error, NOT a sharp stack trace.)
evals.expected_output: Process should exit with non-zero status and print a clear error like "✗ cell extends past image right edge: x+w=400 > width=256". No sharp stack trace should leak to the user.
expectations: Process exits with non-zero status
Error message does NOT contain 'sharp' or 'node_modules'
Error message mentions the cell's key and the bound violation
Error message is a single line, not multi-line stack trace

evals.prompt: I have a sprite sheet at /tmp/eval-inputs/sample-spritesheet.png (768x512) and I want to extract 32 cells in a specific non-grid layout. The map is too big for --map inline, so I'd like to put it in a JSON file. Save the map to /tmp/eval-inputs/big-map.json with 32 cells (use any coordinates that fit, e.g. 8 cols x 4 rows in 96x128 each), then use --map-file /tmp/eval-inputs/big-map.json to extract them, and build the atlas at /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-7/.
evals.expected_output: A 32-frame atlas at /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-7/. The skill should accept --map-file (or equivalent) and extract cells from the JSON file directly without me having to inline the JSON on the command line.
expectations: atlas.png is created
atlas.json contains 32 frame entries (frame_00 through frame_31 or similar)
All 32 source PNGs are extracted to a tmp/ subfolder
The pipeline didn't require inlining a 1KB+ JSON on the command line

evals.prompt: I have a sprite sheet at /tmp/eval-inputs/sample-spritesheet.png (768x512, 3x2 grid). Each cell is 256x256 in the source. I want the extracted cells resized to 64x64 (small, pixel-art style) BEFORE background removal. Save the final clean 64x64 frames to /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-8/.
evals.expected_output: 6 clean 64x64 transparent PNGs at /tmp/eval-outputs/eval-8/. The resize should happen at the extract step so background removal works on the smaller images (faster, cleaner edges).
expectations: All 6 output PNGs are exactly 64x64 pixels
Output PNGs have transparent backgrounds
The shapes are still recognizable (not garbled)
